question_text: An equilateral triangle is divided from the center into 3 equal parts. What is each central angle?

why_wrong_explanations.0: 90° is the central angle of a square (4 sides).
why_wrong_explanations.1: 120° is the central angle of a triangle (3 sides).
why_wrong_explanations.2: 180° is a straight angle, not a central angle of a regular polygon.
why_wrong_explanations.3: 60° is the central angle of a hexagon (6 sides).

question_text: A regular polygon has 12 sides. Find the number of diagonals that can be drawn from one vertex.

why_wrong_explanations.0: 8 diagonals would be for an 11-sided polygon (11 - 3 = 8).
why_wrong_explanations.1: 10 diagonals would be for a 13-sided polygon (13 - 3 = 10).
why_wrong_explanations.2: 11 diagonals would be for a 14-sided polygon (14 - 3 = 11).
